reason: Add session-based integration testing per docs/product-testing-spec.md — new Session/ApplyResult harness, canonical session library (ST-001..ST-083), and request-model property invariants (TC-P012..TC-P014). Amends Design 2 of ADR-018 from the fixture-based Rust harness to sessions built on the request model (ADR-038).

Amend ADR-018 to replace Design 2 (Rust fixture-based Integration Test Harness) with Design 2 (Session-Based Integration Testing built on the request model from ADR-038). Add TC-P012..TC-P014 for request-model property invariants. Update title and phase-assignment table. Follow-on product_adr_amend call will update content-hash and record the amendment audit entry.

Resolve FT-043 W003 (missing exit-criteria) and W004 (invariant/chaos TCs missing formal blocks). Add one exit-criteria TC covering the observable outcome for session-based testing, and insert AISP formal blocks on every invariant/chaos TC so `product graph check` is clean.

Fix E001 formal block parse errors — replace ⟦Λ:Chaos⟧ and ⟦Λ:Property⟧ (not in AISP grammar per src/formal/parser.rs) with ⟦Λ:Scenario⟧ which is the valid block type for given/when/then structured formal blocks.
Link ADR-024 (metrics — stage 4 of pipeline) and ADR-036 (tag-based tracking — stage 5 phase-lock detection) to FT-044 to cover the observability domain gap raised by preflight.
Bulk acknowledge ADR-018 and ADR-043 on features predating the 2026-04-22 cross-cutting scope promotions
Create TC-665..TC-670 for session change operations (ST-010..ST-015)
Back-link TC-665..670 into FT-041, FT-043, ADR-018, ADR-038 tests arrays
Create TC-671..TC-677 for verify, phase-gate, and drift sessions (ST-040..042, ST-050..054)
Back-link TC-671..677 into FT-043 (and the FT-044/FT-045 verify/drift features they also exercise)
Back-link TC-678..680 into FT-043 and the context-bundle features they exercise
Link FT-061 to its dependency FT-057 (this bug-fix builds on the canonical .product/ layout)
Link TC-729 to FT-061 and validate the FT-057 baseline
FT-062: declare feature dependencies on FT-018, FT-021, FT-038, FT-041 — done via request_apply because the very gap this feature reports prevents using a granular MCP tool for depends-on.
Align FT-065 phase with its dependencies (FT-021, FT-061 are phase 5) so the W005 graph-check warning clears.

File the FT-046 follow-on. The MCP tools product_feature_status and
product_test_status are no-op stubs that return a misleading success
envelope without writing the file. Confirmed empirically: 11 TC status
updates via MCP, every file unchanged on disk, graph check still reported
old statuses. The "Use CLI for status updates with full side-effects" note
buried in the payload is the only signal of failure — easy for an LLM (or
human) to miss. Slice functions feature::plan_status_change and
tc::plan_status_change already exist; this feature wires MCP to them.

pipeline). Default phase 1 from creation produced W005 phase-label
disagreement.
